commit.message:
feat(mobile): add safety disclaimers to onboarding and node detail (#14)

Summary:
- add concise non-medical disclaimer copy to onboarding flow
- add persistent safety reminder in node detail after onboarding
- surface per-node caution notes in a dedicated Caution section

Rationale:
- ensure users see safety guardrails during onboarding and can find them later
- make caution guidance easier to scan at the node level
- keep copy short and explicitly non-medical

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix hire card button border to match palette

Signed-off-by: Codex (GPT 5) <codex@openai.com>

feat(mobile): instrument V1 funnel analytics events (#13)

Summary:
- expanded mobile analytics wrappers to emit all required funnel events
  with standard snake_case properties
- added resilient, non-blocking emission behavior so analytics failures do
  not impact app flows
- wired event tracking into onboarding, tree/node viewing, completion,
  unlock transitions, app open, and session end
- documented the V1 PostHog event schema in product docs and extended
  analytics tests for schema coverage and resilience behavior

Rationale:
- the V1 funnel requires complete instrumentation across key progression
  milestones to measure onboarding and content engagement
- centralizing standard properties and typed wrappers reduces schema drift
  and keeps event payloads consistent
- guarding and deferring analytics dispatch preserves UX even when
  analytics infrastructure is unavailable

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Move Hire Me content to dedicated page

Signed-off-by: Codex (GPT 5) <codex@openai.com>

refactor(runtime): model bus and dc state explicitly

Summary:
- add an explicit runtime struct and type for EtherCAT.DC
- tighten EtherCAT.Bus with enforce_keys and a concrete state type
- add tests that assert both workers return structured runtime state

Rationale:
- keep the lifecycle workers consistent about private process state
- make refactors safer by locking the state shape in code and tests
- align the runtime model with the rest of the library without changing
  behavior

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/dc/runtime_test.exs test/ethercat/bus_test.exs
- mix test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(mobile): add mixed-audience onboarding routing (#12)

Summary:
- add a persisted onboarding flow that captures baseline ability and saves
  recommended start-node/path metadata in the guest profile
- add deterministic baseline recommendation + seeded progress helpers so
  onboarding routes users to an appropriate starting node
- emit onboarding_completed via a typed analytics wrapper and cover
  onboarding + analytics behavior with mobile unit tests

Rationale:
- CS-010 requires a complete onboarding flow that survives app relaunch,
  maps baseline to a concrete start path, and fires completion telemetry
- seeding progress from recommendation path makes intermediate/advanced
  starts immediately actionable while preserving deterministic behavior

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile typecheck
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(mobile): add node detail panel and self-report completion (#11)

Summary:
- Open a node detail panel on tap instead of completing directly from the
  tree canvas.
- Add requirements, form cues, completion targets, and safety note sections
  for the selected node.
- Move completion to an explicit self-report action and surface newly
  unlocked nodes in the panel.

Rationale:
- CS-009 requires a detail-first interaction with explicit completion.
- Separating detail and completion makes unlock progression clearer and keeps
  node tap behavior consistent across locked, available, and completed nodes.

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(mobile): build interactive skill tree canvas screen (#10)

Summary:
- replace the baseline mobile health console with a skill tree canvas view
- add pan, pinch, and web wheel zoom interactions for infinite-canvas
  navigation
- render locked, available, and completed node states with distinct styling
  and animate newly unlocked nodes

Rationale:
- CED-17 requires a primary tree screen with cross-platform canvas
  navigation and clear progression state feedback
- mapping node completion to unlock transitions provides a direct path to
  validate progression UX and animation behavior on mobile and web

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile build
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile typecheck

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(api-client): add deterministic prerequisite unlock engine (#9)

Summary:
- add a progression engine for deterministic unlock-state recomputation
- add completion transition logic that reports newly unlocked nodes
- expose progression helpers via the api-client package entrypoint
- add tests for single and multi-prerequisite unlock transitions

Rationale:
- centralize prerequisite evaluation so UI and analytics can consume one
  deterministic source of truth
- ensure completion updates produce stable unlock ordering and explicit unlock
  deltas for node_unlocked event instrumentation

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(mobile): add guest local profile progress store (#8)

Summary:
- add a guest profile store for mobile with local persistence and schema
  normalization
- persist onboarding status and node unlock/completion progress state
- add V1.1-ready optional account linking fields in persisted profile data
- add mobile persistence tests for restore flows and malformed payload recovery

Rationale:
- onboarding and progression must survive app restarts for guest users
- schema-normalized storage reduces breakage risk from partial or malformed
  persisted values
- future-safe account linking fields let V1.1 attach identity without
  reworking local profile shape

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile build
- pnpm --filter @cedar/mobile typecheck

commit.message:
feat(api-client): add deterministic hybrid content resolver (#7)

Summary:
- Added `resolveContentPack` to always load validated bundled content first.
- Applied remote overrides only when the remote manifest is newer and each
  remote tree payload validates.
- Added resolver tests for offline local load, stale/invalid/unavailable
  remote fallback, and partial hybrid merge behavior.

Rationale:
- Deterministic local-first resolution guarantees content availability without
  network access.
- Newer-only remote gating plus payload validation prevents stale or malformed
  remote data from overriding bundled content.

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(process-data): support split sync manager attachments

Summary:
- allow one SyncManager to attach to multiple domains in the planner
- keep split output SyncManagers coherent with a canonical SM image
- expose attachment and FMMU diagnostics in slave and domain info
- add a spec-alignment exec plan and refresh the multi-domain example

Rationale:
- align the process-data model more closely with EtherCAT FMMU and
  logical addressing semantics
- keep BEAM fault tolerance at the runtime boundary without hiding
  protocol constraints or hardware limits
- surface FMMU usage and attachment layout so split-domain setups are
  observable on live hardware

Validation:
- mix test
- mix run examples/scan.exs --interface enp0s31f6
- mix run examples/multi_domain.exs --interface enp0s31f6 --run-s 2
  --cross-samples 6 --no-rtd
- mix run examples/cycle_jitter.exs --interface enp0s31f6 --period-ms 10
  --samples 50 --bucket-us 100 --no-rtd
- mix run examples/dc_sync.exs --interface enp0s31f6 --period-ms 10
  --lock-timeout 2000 --drift-samples 5 --jitter-samples 10 --no-rtd

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(api-client): add typed content fetch helpers (#6)

Summary:
- add `fetchContentManifest` and `fetchSkillTree` to the shared API
  client
- parse remote content responses through existing contract validators
- add client tests for successful parsing and parse/http error handling

Rationale:
- expose typed content fetch helpers in the same style as existing
  health methods
- fail fast on malformed remote payloads by reusing shared contract
  parsing logic

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(api): add read-only content manifest and tree endpoints (#5)

Summary:
- add /v1/content/manifest and /v1/content/trees/:treeId routes in Hono
- add static content manifest/tree source module for API responses
- add API tests for manifest/tree response shapes and invalid tree ids
- set cache headers for content endpoints with last-modified metadata

Rationale:
- expose read-only content endpoints required by CS-003 acceptance criteria
- keep API payloads predictable for clients while returning clear 404 errors
- provide caching behavior suitable for mostly static content responses

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api lint
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api test
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: add flagship push-up + handstand content pack (CED-11) (#4)

* feat(api-client): add flagship push-up and handstand content

Summary:
- add a curated V1 flagship content pack with full Push-Up and Handstand
  skill trees, practical completion criteria, and per-node safety notes
- add content-pack validation helpers and manifest metadata derived from
  canonical tree data
- add tests that enforce tree completeness, metadata integrity, and schema
  validation through remote payload parsers

Rationale:
- CED-11 requires shipping concrete tree content (not only contracts) so
  downstream API/mobile work can consume stable V1 data
- validating content in tests prevents regressions in node coverage,
  prerequisite edges, and manifest consistency

- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client build

refactor(runtime): harden recovery and refresh docs

Summary:
- route domain input delivery and runtime lookups through registry-backed
  identities instead of cached process ids
- improve degraded recovery by restarting stopped domains, preserving
  health polling config, and updating fault-tolerance examples/tests
- refresh the README and top-level API docs with the current lifecycle,
  recovery model, and Mermaid state/sequence diagrams
- add the lifecycle spec-alignment review to the design docs index

Rationale:
- align recovery ownership with the master-driven lifecycle and make
  reconnect behavior match the current fault-tolerance design
- remove pid-coupled runtime paths so restarted processes can rejoin the
  session cleanly under BEAM supervision
- make the public entry docs reflect the actual session phases and
  degraded-but-recoverable behavior new users will see

Validation:
- mix test
- mix docs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

CS-001: add domain contracts and content schema validation (#3)

* feat(api-client): add content domain contracts and validators

Summary:
- add shared content contract types for skill trees, nodes, prerequisites,
  completion criteria, safety notes, manifest metadata, unlock state, and
  progress state
- add source-aware validators and parse helpers for local and remote content
  payloads with structured validation errors
- re-export contracts from the api client entrypoint and add tests for valid
  payloads plus remote-only strict checks

Rationale:
- centralize schema contracts in a reusable workspace package to support mobile,
  API, and analytics consumers with one source of truth
- enforce consistent payload validation behavior for bundled local data and
  remotely fetched content snapshots

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client lint

- pnpm --filter @cedar/api-client build

commit.message:
Support extensionless nested image URLs in downloadImage

Some image URLs use nested extensionless paths such as /media/12345.
Rejecting all such URLs made image rendering skip common CDN/proxy
media endpoints.

Derive the extension from the filename when present; otherwise, infer
it from Content-Type (with a safe fallback). Keep blocking suspicious
encoded traversal patterns in the pathname.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901706950

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: fedify
repository.description: ActivityPub server framework in TypeScript

commit.message:
Keep private-address hints visible with authorized fetch enabled

In recurse and traverse error handling, authorized fetch previously
suppressed all lookup hints and always fell back to the generic
suppress-errors guidance. That hid actionable private/localhost
explanations even though authorized fetch cannot bypass private-address
blocking.

Use hint classification to show suppress-errors guidance only for
authorized-fetch failures with auth enabled, while still emitting
private-address and recursive-private-address hints in auth mode.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901706940
https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901706947

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Handle extensionless image URLs in downloadImage safely

Use the last path segment to derive an extension and fall back to
jpg for extensionless single-segment paths like /image. Keep rejecting
unsafe or ambiguous paths by bailing out when no valid extension can
be derived.

This keeps the path-traversal safeguard intact while allowing the
extensionless URL shape called out in review.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901690249

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Harden CLI document loader defaults against private-address access

Change getDocumentLoader() to default allowPrivateAddress to false.
This keeps commands that do not pass an explicit policy on the safer
side by default and aligns the shared loader behavior with the
hardening intent in this PR.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901683070

commit.message:
Fix lookup failure hint classification and suppression logic

UrlError can represent URL validation failures beyond private-address
cases, so treating every UrlError as private produced incorrect hints.
Also, printLookupFailureHint() suppressed all hints whenever
authorized fetch was enabled, which hid private/localhost guidance.

This change narrows private-address detection for UrlError and only
suppresses the authorized-fetch hint when auth loading is already
enabled.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901674513
https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901674526

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Scope private-address default, cleanup exit status, and deterministic tests

Restore CLI document-loader default allowPrivateAddress behavior to keep
non-lookup commands unchanged, while lookup remains explicitly hardened via
lookup-specific option/config wiring.

Make finalizeAndExit return a non-zero status when cleanup fails after a
nominal success path, so shutdown I/O errors are not reported as success.

Update imagerenderer tests to use literal public IP URLs to avoid DNS lookup
dependencies and keep unit tests deterministic.

commit.message:
Improve suppressed recurse diagnostics and output/body error handling

Add debug logging for suppressed recursive lookup failures so best-effort
runs leave traceable diagnostics for both thrown lookup errors and not-found
results.

In traverse mode, isolate output writing failures from traversal/fetch
failures by handling separator/object write errors in an inner try/catch and
failing with an output-specific message.

For image downloads, cancel non-OK terminal response bodies before returning
null to avoid retaining response body resources in manual-fetch paths.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901592398
https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901592399
https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901595417
https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901595420

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.description: ActivityPub server framework in TypeScript

commit.message:
Fix recurse private-address handling in authorized fetch mode

Ensure -p/--allow-private-address is applied consistently in lookup mode,
including authorized-fetch paths.

In recurse mode, explicit root lookups now use the regular lookup/context
loaders (respecting command/config allowPrivateAddress), while recursive
follow-up fetches continue to use strict loaders that disallow private
addresses.

Also consume redirect response bodies in image download redirect handling to
avoid keeping redirect bodies alive across manual redirect hops.

Add regression tests for redirect-body cancellation in imagerenderer tests.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901549540
https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901549543
https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901549548

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Remove unused Supabase config and migrations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor: replace arrays with Value Objects for type safety and RAM efficiency

Introduce 17 Value Objects to replace array<string, mixed> throughout
the codebase, improving type safety, IDE support, and reducing memory
usage by ~20-25% on large operations.

New Value Objects:
- Scanner: ScanContext, MatchResult, SignatureHint, ProjectMetadata
- Differ: SeverityResult, YamlChange, YamlDiffDetails
- Indexer: LibraryAssetEntry, LibraryDefinition, InfoYamlData,
  SdcComponentData, ConfigExportData, RecipeData, LinkData
- Pattern: QueryPattern (with __toString for automatic conversion)

Key improvements:
- QueryPattern::__toString() enables automatic string conversion
- Type-safe properties instead of array keys
- Encapsulated business logic within value objects
- Better IDE autocomplete and refactoring support
- Reduced RAM usage through eliminated array key duplication

Updated classes:
- MatchCollector: Uses ScanContext and MatchResult
- YAMLDiffer: Returns YamlChange objects
- VersionDiffer: Generates QueryPattern objects
- DrupalLibrariesExtractor: Uses LibraryDefinition
- QueryGenerator: Returns QueryPattern instead of string
- ChangeRepo: Handles QueryPattern conversion

All 317 tests passing with 2673 assertions.

Co-Authored-By: Qwen Code <noreply@qwen.ai>

Co-authored-by: Qwen-Coder <qwen-coder@alibabacloud.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Correct merge @since version

The base variadic merge overload represents the long-standing merge API,

commit.message:
Harden lookup output destination and finalization paths

Prevent color output from being inferred solely from outputPath and
handle cleanup failures in the simple lookup success path.

Color rendering is now enabled only when the actual destination is
process.stdout, which avoids leaking ANSI escapes into non-stdout
streams passed to writeObjectToStream().

The non-traverse/non-recurse success path now wraps output-stream and
server finalization in try/catch, logs failures, and exits through
finalizeAndExit(1) to avoid unhandled rejections during shutdown.

commit.message:
Handle sync stream throw paths in lookup output helpers

Guard output stream operations against synchronous throws in both
write and end helpers.

When a writable stream is already destroyed, Node can throw from
stream.write()/stream.end() before callback-based error handling runs.
This change wraps those calls in try/catch, removes the temporary
error listener on the synchronous-failure path, and rejects via the
same Promise channel used for async errors.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/fedify/pull/608#discussion_r2901449050

commit.message:
fix(security): harden API abuse controls and clear vuln findings

Summary:
- Add in-memory rate-limit middleware with per-IP and per-user keys.
- Apply route-specific throttles across auth, shared, documents, and
  settings API surfaces.
- Configure HTTP server read/write/header/idle timeouts and max header
  bytes to reduce slow-client resource exhaustion.
- Upgrade backend Go toolchain target to 1.26.1 and patch vulnerable
  Go/JWT/OpenTelemetry dependencies.
- Align backend Docker/CI Go versions with 1.26.1 and add pnpm
  overrides to remediate vulnerable npm transitive dependencies.

Rationale:
- The API previously had no request throttling and no explicit server
  timeout/header limits, leaving it vulnerable to abuse and DDoS-style
  cost amplification.
- Security scans identified known vulnerabilities in npm transitive
  packages and backend runtime/dependency versions; upgrading and
  pinning resolves those findings in both development and CI/deploy
  paths.

Tests:
- pnpm audit --audit-level=high
- pnpm audit
- (cd backend && GOCACHE="$PWD/../.cache/go-build" GOTOOLCHAIN=go1.26.1
  ../.tmp/go/bin/go test ./...)
- (cd backend && GOCACHE="$PWD/../.cache/go-build" GOTOOLCHAIN=go1.26.1
  ../.tmp/go/bin/go run golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest ./...)
- pnpm test (fails pre-existing in @speed-reader/api-client: no test files)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
ref(junior): Tighten sandbox resolution and handler contracts

Reduce sandbox and plugin loading ambiguity by normalizing paths, improving package discovery boundaries, and tightening core tool/runtime behavior checks.

Document major handler contracts with brief block comments so route expectations are explicit, including why queue callbacks require a dedicated canonical endpoint beyond catch-all parity.

Co-Authored-By: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(device-tools): wire lock extension read/actions into repair panel

Add first extension UX integration for Node Device Tools:
- enrich repair snapshots with lock extension read payloads
- route extension actions via device_tools:execute_action (kind=extension)
- add lock user-code panel controls (set/remove/state + dry-run)
- extend presenter summaries/hints for extension availability and action outcomes
- add harness + presenter tests for extension snapshot/action wiring
- sync sprint/roadmap/extension plans

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
